title: This is the current state of the TSA at Logan Airport
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: TSA agents at Logan Airport continue to work despite missing paychecks due to the government shutdown.
About 30 TSA employees in New England have quit, mostly from Logan Airport, amid low morale and financial strain.
Massport tests new camera system to estimate TSA wait times, unrelated to current government shutdown issues.

title: Man found dead in Brockton was in fight, then run over, DA says
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Lianes Brandao, 28, was found dead after a fight and hit-and-run in Brockton early Sunday.
Brandao was discovered by police in a parking lot and died later at a Boston hospital.
A gray 2013 Jeep Compass was identified in the incident, and its operator has been interviewed.

title: Pregnant, battling cancer — now she’s running the 2026 Boston Marathon
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Jennifer Capone Haberek, from New Hampshire, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon after overcoming cancer during pregnancy.
Haberek was diagnosed with triple-negative breast cancer while eight months pregnant, facing treatment challenges during this period.
She is participating in the marathon to support the Ellie Fund, which helped her family during her cancer battle.

title: A Korean trio of restaurants heads to Allston, starting with Sanbada
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Sanbada, a Korean restaurant, is now open in Allston at 165 Brighton Ave, focusing on Korean-style fish sets.
Bombbada, a late-night Korean eatery, will launch in July, followed by Jinjee, a Korean BBQ, in mid-April.
Sanbada offers a menu featuring grilled fish and banchan, priced between $22 and $30, and stays open until 10 p.m.

title: The 5 most exciting books to read this spring — what’s on your list?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Rosie Hewlett's "Sweetbitter Song" reimagines "Odyssey" characters in a historical, sapphic love story, released March 17, 2026.
Tara Menon's "Under Water" explores friendship loss and survival over decades, with a release date of March 17, 2026.
David George Haskell's "How Flowers Made Our World" examines the role of flowers in evolution, available March 24, 2026.

title: Vermont Green FC upset Portland Hearts of Pine in regional rivalry U.S. Open Cup clash
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Vermont Green FC defeated Portland Hearts of Pine 1-0 in a U.S. Open Cup match in Burlington, Vermont.
David Ismail scored the winning goal in the 40th minute, securing Vermont's upset victory over Portland.
Vermont Green FC will advance to the second round of the U.S. Open Cup, opponents to be determined.

title: Police arrest 15-year-old in East Boston stabbing after he allegedly returns to the crime scene for his cellphone
author: John Waller
bullets: Police arrested a 15-year-old in East Boston for allegedly returning to a stabbing scene to retrieve his cellphone.
A witness intervened by disarming one suspect during an attempted robbery near the Maverick T stop in East Boston.
The teen faces charges of aggravated assault and armed and masked robbery; the victim's injuries were non-life-threatening.

title: Woman, 45, killed in Newton crash
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A 45-year-old woman died after her vehicle struck a tree in a two-car crash in Newton.
The crash occurred around 4:30 p.m. near Walnut Street and Judith Road, according to officials.
The second driver involved did not suffer serious injuries; the incident is under investigation.

title: 1 dead after car crashes into tree in Chelmsford
author: John Waller
bullets: A fatal car crash occurred at 12:30 p.m. on Tuesday in Chelmsford, according to local police.
The driver crashed into a tree near 75 Littleton Road and was pronounced dead at a local hospital.
The victim has not been publicly identified, and the incident is under investigation by authorities.

title: Local obituary: Janet Swallow, 68, Danvers resident and longtime Lahey nurse
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: Janet M. Swallow, 68, of Danvers, passed away at her home on March 12, 2026.
Swallow dedicated over 30 years as a nurse at Lahey Medical Center in Burlington.
Visiting hours are scheduled for March 22, 2026, at C.R. Lyons & Sons Funeral Directors in Danvers.
